results.confidence_notes: ChatGPT / GPT-4 launches trigger AI capex cycle (2023 Q1–Q2). NVDA +120% YTD by May 2023. Data centre, semiconductor, power demand themes emerge. AI capex growth is the dominant structural tailwind. This is the scenario the Sprint 4A/B engine is best designed to express.

conditional_rules_checked: rate_cut_direction: not expressible in current driver state vocabulary — both rising and easing rates map to watch_shadow_inferred
selective_risk_on: current posture logic returns cautious when credit=watch + risk_appetite=watch + no active driver; risk-on nuance not expressible
engine_limitations: Driver state vocabulary has no directionality (active/watch only) — 'easing rates' cannot be distinguished from 'still-high rates at watch'
liquidity driver unavailable — improving liquidity conditions not expressible
Regime defaulting to credit_stress_watch_shadow is a known Sprint 4A/B limitation for watch-only scenarios; Sprint 5B fixture documents this gap for Sprint 6 resolution
results.confidence_notes: Fed signals/begins cutting rates (Sep 2024 first cut). Selective risk-on. AI capex cycle ongoing. Credit broadly contained. Liquidity improving. Limitation: driver state vocab has no 'easing' direction — 'watch' used as best approximation for transitioning/declining rates. Regime may appear cautious rather than risk-on due to vocabulary constraint.

warnings: Historical replay uses date-anchored manual fixtures only. Driver states are approximations of known historical conditions using the Sprint 4A/B vocabulary. Not a validation against real market data.

Scenarios with regime=credit_stress_watch_shadow (e.g. 2024_rate_cut_pivot_selective_risk_on) reflect a known engine limitation: the posture logic cannot distinguish 'easing rates at watch' from 'credit stress at watch' when all active drivers are absent.
